cmyk_color_chart.description: Standard black is used for text, while rich black is recommended for large background areas to prevent a washed-out look.

colors.name: Standard Black
colors.notes: Use for text and fine details.

colors.name: Rich Black
colors.notes: Use for large solid backgrounds. Do not set all values to 100 to avoid over-saturation.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Greys can be created using only the Black (K) channel for a safe, perfectly neutral result. Rich greys use a mix of CMYK for deeper, warmer, or cooler tones, but require careful balancing to avoid unwanted colour shifts.

colors.name: Light Neutral Grey
colors.notes: Safe, neutral light grey using only black ink. Perfect for subtle backgrounds.

colors.name: Medium Neutral Grey
colors.notes: Standard mid-tone grey. No risk of colour casting.

colors.name: Dark Neutral Grey
colors.notes: Dark, flat grey. Good for deep shadows without using rich black.

colors.name: Cool Rich Grey
colors.notes: A deeper grey with a slight blue/steely undertone. Cyan is slightly higher than Magenta and Yellow.

colors.name: Warm Rich Grey
colors.notes: A deeper grey with a slight brownish/warm undertone. Magenta and Yellow are higher than Cyan.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Watch out for magenta and yellow levels. Too much magenta turns it pink; too much yellow turns it orange.

colors.name: Vibrant Red
colors.notes: Standard pure CMYK red.

colors.name: Deep Red
colors.notes: A darker, richer red without turning muddy.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Combine two parts yellow and one part magenta for a bright orange.

colors.name: Bright Orange
colors.notes: Balanced formula for vibrant orange.

colors.name: Earthy Brown
colors.notes: A balanced, warm brown.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Muted, natural tones are incredibly popular in modern streetwear. These require careful balancing of all four channels to achieve a dusty look.

colors.name: Olive Drab
colors.notes: Classic military/streetwear olive green. High yellow and cyan with enough black to mute it.

colors.name: Terracotta
colors.notes: Rusty, burnt orange-red. Earthy and warm.

colors.name: Sand / Khaki
colors.notes: Warm beige. Great as an accent colour against dark fabrics.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Equal parts Cyan and Yellow make vibrant greens. Yellow should be handled carefully to avoid mustard tones.

colors.name: Pure Yellow
colors.notes: Maximum yellow density.

colors.name: Vibrant Green
colors.notes: Equal parts Cyan and Yellow.

cmyk_color_chart.description: High-impact cool tones. These rely heavily on Cyan and Yellow while keeping Magenta strictly at zero to prevent the colours from looking muddy or turning into a dull blue.

colors.name: Bright Aqua
colors.notes: Punchy tropical blue-green. Great for summer apparel.

colors.name: Deep Teal
colors.notes: Rich, jewel-toned teal. The slight magenta and black add depth without destroying vibrancy.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Blue is challenging in CMYK. Keep mixtures balanced to avoid purple or green tints.

colors.name: Balanced Blue
colors.notes: Reliable print blue.

colors.name: Pure Cyan
colors.notes: Standard pure CMYK cyan.

colors.name: Navy Blue
colors.notes: Deep, strong dark blue.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Regal purple tones are CMYK friendly using a 3:2 magenta to cyan ratio.

colors.name: Regal Purple
colors.notes: Magenta-heavy purple.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Pinks rely on Magenta. Keep cyan, yellow, and black low for true pinks.

colors.name: Hot Pink / Pure Magenta
colors.notes: Maximum magenta density.

colors.name: Soft Pink
colors.notes: A lighter pastel pink variation.

cmyk_color_chart.description: Pastels look incredible in DTF because they utilise the bright white ink base layer. Keep your C, M, and Y values under 30% for the best soft-touch effect.

colors.name: Mint Green
colors.notes: Crisp, cool pastel green.

colors.name: Soft Lavender
colors.notes: Gentle floral purple. Zero yellow keeps it cool and clean.

colors.name: Peach
colors.notes: Warm, soft pastel. Popular on modern graphic tees.
